title: Topic, Headers, and Alternate Routing · Runtime Contract: Headers Exchange Contract
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must parse topic segments, match bindings, model headers, and separate alternate custody from mandatory returns; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the headers exchange contract increment in a deterministic Go routing and unroutable-decision table. Learners prog…
objectives: Use headers exchanges only with typed bounded metadata and explicit all-versus-any matching, not as a schema-free routing escape hatch; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Enumerate overlapping topic bindings and prove which queues receive one copy even when multiple bindings on the same queue match; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
